  {
    "id": "s01",
    "title": "Town's giant pumpkin smashes state record at fall fair",
    "summary": "A retired schoolteacher's 2,412-pound pumpkin took the blue ribbon at the county fair, edging last year's champion. The grower credits an experimental compost blend and a summer of relentless watering. Organizers say attendance hit a ten-year high as families lined up for photos. The pumpkin will be hollowed into a boat for the annual regatta on the millpond next month."
  },
  {
    "id": "s02",
    "title": "Second-graders raise a therapy-dog reading squad",
    "summary": "An elementary school's new program pairs shy young readers with certified therapy dogs, and teachers say confidence has soared. The dogs sit patiently while children sound out tricky words. The waitlist to join now stretches into next semester. One parent called the weekly session the highlight of her daughter's entire school week."
  },
  {
    "id": "s03",
    "title": "Fatal interstate pileup claims three student athletes",
    "summary": "A chain-reaction crash in dense fog on the interstate killed three members of a college track team and injured a dozen travelers. Investigators are examining whether a jackknifed truck triggered it. The university opened a counseling center and postponed the weekend's meets. The team's coach asked that the community keep the injured runners in their thoughts."
  },
  {
    "id": "s04",
    "title": "Diner celebrates 75 years with a nickel-coffee day",
    "summary": "The oldest diner in town rolled prices back to 1950 for a morning, serving five-cent coffee to a line around the block. The owner, whose grandfather opened the counter, wanted to thank the regulars who kept the booths full through three generations. The griddle never cooled before noon, and the cook says his feet have not forgiven him yet."

    "id": "s05",
    "title": "Amateur astronomer photographs a rare green comet",
    "summary": "A hobbyist with a backyard telescope captured a striking image of a comet not visible to the naked eye, and skywatching groups have shared it widely. He spent four cold nights aligning his rig before the clouds broke. Science teachers plan to use the photo in class. He plans to livestream the comet's closest pass so the whole town can watch from home."

    "id": "s06",
    "title": "Two workers killed in warehouse fire on the waterfront",
    "summary": "A fast-moving fire tore through a riverside warehouse overnight, killing two employees unable to reach an exit. Crews fought the blaze for hours as stored chemicals fed the flames. Investigators are examining an electrical fault; counselors were made available to survivors. Coworkers left flowers at the warehouse gate as investigators combed the gutted interior."

The students coded the vision system over lunch periods. The district is weighing a cafeteria pilot next year. Judges praised the design's simplicity and asked the students to demonstrate it twice."

    "title": "Rescued shelter cat elected honorary town mayor",
    "summary": "Residents cast penny-a-vote ballots to name a one-eyed shelter cat honorary mayor, raising thousands for the shelter. The cat now holds court in a tiny City Hall bed and signs proclamations with an inked paw. Adoptions have tripled since the campaign began. A local sign-maker donated a tiny brass nameplate for the cat's City Hall door."

    "summary": "Torrential rain sent a wall of water through a low-lying neighborhood before dawn, and officials confirmed several deaths with more residents unaccounted for. Rescue teams pulled survivors from rooftops as the river crested at a record. The governor declared an emergency. Volunteers formed a sandbag line as forecasters warned of a second crest by nightfall."

    "title": "Retiree knits tiny sweaters for cold-snap penguins",
    "summary": "A ninety-year-old former nurse has knitted hundreds of small wool sweaters for a wildlife center that rehabilitates seabirds. She works through three balls of yarn a week and recruited a dozen friends. The center says the garments keep recovering birds warm. The center says the sweaters also make the recovering birds far easier to spot and count."

The ocean-tides show drew a standing ovation and near-perfect drill marks. Boosters are already fundraising for next year's buses. The drum line stayed an extra hour after the trophy ceremony just to soak it in."

    "summary": "A powerful earthquake flattened rows of older buildings in a historic district, trapping an unknown number of people under rubble. Rescuers worked through the night by floodlight as aftershocks rattled the ruins. Officials warned the toll is likely to climb. Aid convoys idled at the edge of the district, unable to reach the worst of the rubble."

Staff say the tomato drawer has become a swap-meet of advice. A children's plot grew enough zucchini to donate crates. Master gardeners now hold a monthly clinic beside the seed drawers for first-time growers."

The captain has started keeping a spare 'rescue hat' behind the wheelhouse for such occasions."

The count has passed forty, and a nearby class walks over weekly to sketch. A retired entomologist visits on Fridays to help the children identify what they have found."

Rescue crews inched across the ice to reach crushed vehicles. Officials urged drivers to stay home as the freezing rain continued. The bridge stayed closed for hours as crews chipped stranded vehicles from the ice."
